common.title: Bonds Calculator
common.description: Professional calculator for Polish treasury bonds.
common.calculation_in_progress: Calculation in progress. Recalculation only ends once the scenario is fully modeled.
common.initial_calculation_hint: Run the scenario once to see the full result.
common.recalculation_hint: Inputs changed. Recalculate only when the scenario is ready.

site.twitter_description: Focused calculator and reference app for Polish treasury bonds.

saved_scenarios.title: Saved scenarios
saved_scenarios.description: Resume, duplicate, or clean up your last planning runs.
saved_scenarios.empty: No saved scenarios yet. Save current inputs to build reusable planning paths.

strategy_hints.title: Smart Strategy Hints
strategy_hints.edo_better_than_coi: Given your horizon (> 4 years) and projected inflation, EDO (10-year) bonds might be more beneficial than COI (4-year) due to higher margins and annual interest capitalization.
strategy_hints.coi_better_than_edo_short: For a horizon under 4 years, COI bonds offer better liquidity and lower early redemption fees than EDO.
strategy_hints.tax_efficiency_ike: You are paying significant Belka tax ({amount}). Consider using an IKE or IKZE account to keep these funds for yourself.
strategy_hints.ots_repeated: Do you often renew short-term OTS bonds? At current NBP rates, ROR (1-year) or DOR (2-year) bonds might offer higher returns with similar liquidity.
strategy_hints.diversification_hint: Your investment is concentrated in one bond type. Consider diversifying with inflation-indexed bonds (COI/EDO) to protect your purchasing power.
strategy_hints.ike_limit_warning: Your investment amount exceeds the annual IKE/IKZE limit. The surplus will be taxed at the standard 19% rate.

sidebar.workspace_desc: Choose active portfolio before saving new bond lots.
sidebar.recovery_lab_notice: These pages remain reachable, but they are not part of the main calculator path.
sidebar.recovery_scope_desc: The main path stays on single-bond, comparison, ladder, regular-investment, notebook, and data reference pages.

freshness.text_fresh: Core pages are reading current metadata.
freshness.text_partial: Some data may still be fallback coverage.

ladder.description: Test maturity spacing, rollover timing, and liquidity shape for one ladder setup.

shared_portfolio.title: Shared Portfolio

education.subtitle: Understand the mechanisms of the Polish treasury bond market and make informed investment decisions.
education.concepts_subtitle: Key terms essential to understanding your investments.
education.a_inflation: Inflation-indexed bonds (e.g., COI or EDO) have a fixed rate in the first year. In subsequent years, the interest rate is calculated as the inflation rate from the previous year plus a fixed margin.
education.a_tax: Profits from bonds are subject to a 19% capital gains tax (Belka tax) in Poland. The tax is automatically deducted when interest is paid or the bond is redeemed.
education.a_early_exit: Yes, you can request early redemption. A fixed fee is charged for each bond. The fee is deducted from the accrued interest.
education.q_monthly_payout: How do monthly payout bonds work?
education.a_monthly_payout: Bonds like ROR and DOR pay interest every month directly to your account. This interest is not added to the principal (no capitalization).

inflation.title: Inflation
inflation.desc: The increase in prices of goods and services in the economy. For an investor, it means a decrease in the purchasing power of money.

margin.title: Margin
margin.desc: A fixed addition to the inflation rate or the NBP reference rate, guaranteeing a return above these indicators.

capitalization.title: Capitalization
capitalization.desc: The process of adding earned interest to the initial capital. In subsequent periods, interest is calculated on a larger amount.

belka_tax.title: Belka Tax
belka_tax.desc: A flat capital gains tax in Poland, currently 19%.

early_redemption.title: Early Redemption
early_redemption.desc: The possibility of withdrawing funds before the bond's maturity date for a fee specified in the emission letter.

education.disclaimer: Note: Bond conditions may change over time. Always verify the current parameters on the official Polish government bond website (obligacjeskarbowe.pl).

glossary.savings_goal: The amount you want to accumulate by the end of your investment. The calculator helps determine how much you need to invest today to reach this goal.
glossary.capitalization: The process of adding earned interest to the principal, so that in the next period, interest is calculated on a larger amount (compound interest).
glossary.inflation_indexed: Bonds whose interest rate changes every year based on the inflation rate plus a fixed margin (e.g., COI, EDO).
glossary.real_value: The value of your capital adjusted for inflation (purchasing power). It shows how many today's goods you will be able to buy with that money in the future.
glossary.early_withdrawal: A fee for withdrawing funds before the maturity date. It is deducted from the accrued interest, never from the principal capital.
glossary.tax_wrapper: Special retirement accounts that allow you to avoid Belka tax if certain conditions are met (e.g., reaching age 60 for IKE).

bonds.gross_value_desc: Total value of your investment including all interest earned, before any taxes or early withdrawal fees are deducted.
bonds.net_profit_desc: Your total net earnings after deducting Belka tax and any early withdrawal fees. This is the actual growth of your wealth.
bonds.real_cagr_desc: The average annual real return of your investment after adjusting for the loss of purchasing power due to inflation.
bonds.breakdown_desc: Year-by-year summary including capital growth and taxes.
bonds.error_100_pln: Must be a multiple of 100 PLN
bonds.error_future_date: Date cannot be in the future
bonds.regular_calc_desc: Simulation of building a portfolio through regular purchases.
bonds.lots_desc: Each bond purchase is tracked as a separate lot with its own maturity date.
bonds.regular_calc_info: In this simulation, each subsequent lot of bonds is purchased on the same day of each month/quarter. Calculations account for the fact that lots purchased at the end of the period will have less time to earn a profit.
bonds.is_rebought_desc: Buying new bonds using funds from maturing ones.
bonds.early_exit_desc: Fee of {fee} PLN per bond applies if cashed before maturity.
bonds.chart_value_note: Nominal and real lines are the primary reading. Inflation and NBP lines only provide context.
bonds.explanation_inflation: Calculations use the expected inflation rate for indexing. Real value is adjusted for cumulative inflation to show today's purchasing power.
bonds.explanation_tax: The 19% Belka tax is calculated on earned interest. For capitalized bonds, it's deducted at the end or upon withdrawal.
bonds.monthly_liquidity_desc: Once the ladder cycle completes, this is the average monthly amount that will be unlocked for reinvestment or consumption.
bonds.net_passive_income_desc: This represents the net gain (interest after tax) generated by your ladder every month on top of your original principal.
bonds.ladder_tip_desc: When a bond matures, you receive the full capital plus interest. Reinvesting these funds into a new bond of the same type continues the ladder, ensuring constant liquidity.
bonds.family_bond_notice: Family-bond scenarios only make sense if the household eligibility rule actually applies.
bonds.shared_scenario_snapshot: This is a saved, replayable snapshot of one committed calculation.

form.main_path_desc: Main input path only. Advanced assumptions stay collapsed until you need them.
form.step_core_desc: Choose one bond, set amount, then decide whether you are targeting payout or goal.
form.step_timing_desc: Commit purchase date, horizon, withdrawal timing, and tax wrapper before you calculate.
form.advanced_desc: Inflation assumptions, NBP path, chart display, and other secondary controls.

bonds.use_tax_limit_desc: Split investment if it exceeds IKE/IKZE limit (overflow taxed at 19%).
bonds.savings_notice: Your tax wrapper saved you {amount} compared to a standard account.

simulation.ready_desc: Complete the left-side inputs first, keep advanced assumptions collapsed unless necessary, then run one clean calculation and inspect the summary before going deeper.
simulation.results_stable: Results stay stable until you intentionally rerun with new committed inputs.
simulation.stale_results: Inputs changed. Results below are stale until you rerun the calculation.
simulation.fix_blocking: Fix blocking inputs first, then calculate.
simulation.how_to_read_desc: Read the headline result first, then inspect the chart, and only then move into the detailed timeline and calculation trace.
simulation.chart_section_desc: The chart gives the overall shape of the scenario before you need to read the full timeline.
simulation.chart_help_desc: Start by comparing the nominal and real value lines. Inflation and NBP lines are only supporting context and should not outweigh the investment value path.
simulation.timeline_section_desc: The timeline shows scenario checkpoints: when the rate changes, when interest is credited, and what value remains at each stage.
simulation.meta_desc: Assumptions, notes, and data-quality flags should stay visible without overpowering the result.

reading_guide.summary_first: Read the net payout and profit summary first.
reading_guide.chart_then_timeline: Use the chart for the quick shape of the scenario, and use the timeline only for detailed verification.
reading_guide.stale_until_recalculated: If you change inputs, the old results remain visible until you intentionally recalculate.

simulation.click_calculate: Click 'Calculate Results' to see the projection

primary.title: Primary inputs
primary.desc: Bond type, amount, and target mode.

timing.title: Timing and tax
timing.desc: Purchase date, horizon, withdrawal, and wrapper.

advanced.title: Optional advanced
advanced.desc: Inflation, NBP path, and chart display only if needed.

regular.ready_title: Build one recurring plan, then run one committed simulation.
regular.ready_desc: Set contribution amount, frequency, horizon, and bond type first. Advanced assumptions stay secondary, and no background recalculation runs while you edit.
regular.click: Click 'Simulate Investment' to see the projection
regular.how_it_works: Regular investments track each monthly purchase as a separate lot. This simulation calculates the interest, taxes, and fees for each lot individually based on its own maturity timeline.

timing.title: Timing path
timing.desc: Purchase date, horizon, withdrawal mode, and tax wrapper.

advanced.title: Optional advanced
advanced.desc: Inflation path, rollover behavior, and chart display only if needed.

regular_simulation.ready_title: Build one recurring plan, then run one committed simulation.
regular_simulation.ready_desc: Set contribution amount, frequency, horizon, and bond type first. Advanced assumptions stay secondary until you actually need them.

plan.title: Contribution plan
plan.desc: Amount, cadence, and bond type for the recurring path.

timing.title: Timing path
timing.desc: Start date, horizon, withdrawal mode, and tax wrapper.

advanced.title: Optional advanced
advanced.desc: Inflation, rollover, and display assumptions only when needed.

bonds.wait_capitalization_desc: You are withdrawing just before a major interest payout or capitalization. Waiting a few more days could significantly increase your net profit by up to {amount}.

rate_context.floating_narrative: The first month uses the active issued offer rate. Later months move onto the NBP reference rate {margin}.
rate_context.indexed_narrative: The first year uses the issued offer rate. Later years move onto inflation CPI {margin}.
rate_context.fixed_narrative: This bond keeps the issued offer rate mechanics from the selected offer without switching to a floating reference.

bonds.actual_cash_in_hand: The actual cash you get in hand.
bonds.interest_indexed_desc: Inflation-indexed: Rate = Inflation + Margin. Applied to nominal value.
bonds.capitalization_desc: Capitalization: Earned interest is added to the principal for the next period.
bonds.belka_desc: Belka tax: 19% is deducted from interest earned in each period or at withdrawal for capitalized bonds.
bonds.interest_formula: Interest = Nominal × Rate × (Days in Period / Days in Year)
bonds.rounding_desc: All interest is calculated per individual bond (100 PLN) and rounded to the nearest penny (0.01 PLN).
bonds.tax_rounding_desc: Taxes are withheld according to Polish law, with rounding applied at the moment of payout.
bonds.ike_benefit: IKE wrapper saved approximately {amount} in Belka tax.
bonds.explanation_text: These projections are based on current bond terms and your selected macro-economic assumptions.

community.anonymized_notice: Based on anonymized data from other users' notebooks.

results.summary_description: This is the final net payout for the currently committed scenario. Start with the four metrics below, then go deeper only if you need detail.
results.narrative_maturity: This run holds the bond to the modeled maturity path, so the final payout reflects the full planned cycle.
results.narrative_early_exit: This run assumes early redemption before full maturity, so fees or lost compounding may reduce the payout.
results.wrapper_limit_description: {wrapperAmount} stayed inside the wrapper, while {standardAmount} spilled into a standard account.
results.scenario_facts_description: The minimum set of facts needed to read this run correctly.

audit.title: Calculation for {period}
audit.description: This is a condensed view of one period. It should explain where the result comes from without flooding you with table chrome.

value_meaning.withdrawal: This row shows the exit value after modeled fees and tax at this point.
value_meaning.maturity: This row closes the full bond cycle.
value_meaning.paid_out_cash: This checkpoint combines bond value with cash that has already been paid out along the way.
value_meaning.retained_interest: This checkpoint keeps earned interest inside the bond. No cash has been paid out yet.
value_meaning.default: This is a checkpoint showing value after accrual for this period.

schedule.summary_note: Each row is a scenario checkpoint, not a separate purchase. Read left to right to see what happens, what basis was used, and what value leaves the bond at that point.
schedule.mobile_sheet_description: Open the checkpoint-by-checkpoint schedule in a mobile-friendly view.

scenario_descriptions.low: Lower CPI assumptions soften future indexed coupons and improve real-value context.
scenario_descriptions.base: Base CPI keeps the standard projected path for indexed coupons after the opening period.
scenario_descriptions.high: Higher CPI assumptions increase future indexed coupons but also increase purchasing-power drag.

market_assumptions.indexed_context: For indexed bonds, CPI changes projected coupons after the first period and also changes real-value readouts.
market_assumptions.floating_context: For floating bonds, the first period stays on the issued offer. Later periods follow the projected NBP path plus the bond margin.
market_assumptions.real_value_context: For fixed-rate bonds, inflation changes real purchasing-power readouts but does not change the coupon path.
market_assumptions.non_indexed_note: This bond is not CPI-indexed. Inflation here is used for real-value context, not coupon resets.
market_assumptions.nbp_note: This projected NBP path drives later floating-rate periods after the issued first-period offer ends.

landing.hero_description: A comprehensive toolkit designed for investors matching exact Ministry of Finance logic. Project yields, compare inflation scenarios, and build optimal portfolios.

recovery_home.title: Use the core bond calculator first. Keep the rest secondary.
recovery_home.description: This home page acts as a calm route hub. Start with the flagship single-bond calculator, then move into education, reference data, or narrower strategy tools only when needed.
recovery_home.core_route_desc: The main path is simple: understand bond rules, run one calculator cleanly, then review results without extra dashboard noise.
recovery_home.portfolio_empty_title: Portfolio snapshot appears only after you add real lots.
recovery_home.portfolio_empty_desc: Until then, keep the home page light and use the single calculator for scenario work. Notebook stays available, but it should not compete with the core flow.
recovery_home.notebook_card: Track actual bond lots and revisit saved portfolio context after you finish core simulations.

cards.single_calculator: Run detailed simulations for individual Polish treasury bonds over their entire lifecycle.
cards.comparison: Compare multiple bonds side-by-side to find the best yield for your timeline.
cards.regular_investment: Simulate monthly or yearly contributions into bonds and watch your wealth grow.
cards.ladder: Build a bond ladder strategy to ensure consistent liquidity and optimal interest rates.
cards.economic_data: Track historical inflation and NBP interest rates that affect floating-rate bonds.
cards.education: Learn the basics of Polish treasury bonds, taxation, and investment strategies.

landing.no_events_30d: No major events in the next 30 days.

notifications.title: Notification Center
notifications.empty: No new notifications. Portfolio looks quiet.

since_last_visit.title: Since Last Visit
since_last_visit.suggested_action_events: Review upcoming maturities and rollover options.
since_last_visit.suggested_action_no_events: Refresh a saved scenario or add a new portfolio lot.

recovery_lab.title: Side-lab surfaces
recovery_lab.description: Optimizer, multi-asset history, and retirement withdrawal modeling remain available, but they stay intentionally separated from the core tool list.
recovery_lab.usage_hint: Open the side lab only when you want to inspect narrower or more assumption-sensitive pages on purpose.

recent_work.label: Recent work
recent_work.title: Return to one saved scenario.
recent_work.description: Saved scenarios should act as a quick resume point, not a separate dashboard.
recent_work.empty: No local saved scenarios yet. The home page should not pretend to be a control center when nothing is saved.

start_here.title: Simple primary path
start_here.description: This page should route you into the main tools while keeping the first step calm and obvious.

secondary_tools.title: Secondary tools
secondary_tools.description: These pages remain available, but they should be visited only after the primary scenario flow.

landing.hero_trust_note: Start with one clean calculation before moving into comparisons, notebook work, or side scenarios.

hero_trust_strip.item_1: One primary calculation at a time
hero_trust_strip.item_3: Strategies only after the baseline result

learn-rules.title: Learn the rules
learn-rules.description: Start with education or go straight to the single calculator if you already know the bond type and horizon.

run-one.title: Run one calculation
run-one.description: Enter one committed scenario, calculate it cleanly, and read the result without dashboard side panels.

expand-later.title: Expand only after that
expand-later.description: Comparison, recurring investing, ladder strategy, and notebook should stay secondary, not first.

admin.title: Sync Health Dashboard
admin.subtitle: Real-time data freshness and system synchronization monitor
admin.enter_secret: Enter SYNC_SECRET to view system status
admin.sync_confirm: Are you sure you want to trigger a {mode}? This may take a few minutes.
admin.sync_progress: Manual synchronization in progress... please do not close this window.

inventory.title: Data Health Inventory
inventory.subtitle: Detailed status and freshness of financial data series
inventory.empty: No data series found. Click "Trigger Manual Sync" to initialize the database.

recovery_lab_page.title: Side Lab
recovery_lab_page.description: Limited and experimental calculator surfaces kept reachable outside the main product path.
recovery_lab_page.description_long: These surfaces remain reachable, but they are not part of the primary calculator promise. Each one is narrower, more assumption-sensitive, or more data-dependent than the core flows.
recovery_lab_page.why_separated_desc: The app emphasizes core calculator and reference pages first. Side-lab pages stay available for investigation and iterative validation, but they should not compete with the flagship calculator flows until their scope and evidence improve further.
recovery_lab_page.card_support_notice: Treat this as supporting analysis, not primary calculator flow.
recovery_lab_page.card_notice: Open this page only when you want to inspect a narrower or weaker-support scenario surface on purpose.
recovery_lab_page.what_stays_core_desc: The product stays stronger when the main pages remain simpler and easier to trust.
recovery_lab_page.core_emphasis: Primary emphasis remains on education, single calculator, comparison, regular investment, ladder, notebook, and data reference pages.
recovery_lab_page.core_improve_later: Side-lab pages can still improve later, but they do not define product readiness right now.

optimize.title: Scenario Ranking
optimize.description: Assumption-sensitive payout sorting. Useful as a supporting scenario view, not as a recommendation surface.

multi_asset.title: Historical Reference Comparison
multi_asset.description: Mixed-series comparison with narrower trust than the core bond calculators. Keep it in reference mode only.

retirement.title: Withdrawal Model
retirement.description: Limited steady-rate withdrawal scenario. Narrower than a normal retirement planner and intentionally scoped down.

ladder_page.page_notice_title: Ladder strategy stays useful only when cash-flow spacing matters.
ladder_page.page_notice_desc: Use this page after one clean single-bond run. Ladder logic should support a specific liquidity plan, not replace the main calculator.
ladder_page.empty_state_title: Build one maturity schedule, then inspect liquidity spacing.
ladder_page.empty_state_description: This page answers a narrow question: when capital returns and how concentrated those maturity windows are. Set the plan, run one committed calculation, then inspect the ladder timing.

empty_cards.plan_description: Bond type, amount, frequency, and horizon.
empty_cards.committed_description: Run one explicit schedule instead of recalculating while editing.
empty_cards.timing_description: Check month clustering and cash-return concentration.

ladder_page.empty_state_footer: Results stay stable until you intentionally rerun with a new ladder setup.

reading_guide.check_peak_month: Find the peak maturity month first so you know where liquidity clusters.
reading_guide.check_clustering: Use the share metrics to decide whether the ladder is too concentrated in one window.

ladder_page.stale_results: Inputs changed. The maturity ladder below still shows the previous calculation. Recalculate when you want a fresh schedule.
ladder_page.how_to_read_description: Check peak month, clustering, and maturity spacing before making liquidity decisions.
ladder_page.calculation_context_description: Warnings, assumptions, and freshness context for the ladder engine.

timeline.description: This view shows when your ladder returns capital and how tightly those months cluster.
timeline.narrative: Use the schedule to decide whether your ladder spreads liquidity evenly or bunches too much capital into one window.
timeline.average_maturity_value_description: Typical maturity value across active ladder months.
timeline.spread_gap_description: How much a typical maturity month differs from one recurring contribution.
timeline.active_window_description: Range from the earliest to the latest maturity month in this ladder.
timeline.chart_description: Each bar shows how much net value returns in a given maturity month.
timeline.chart_note_title: How to read the ladder chart
timeline.chart_note_description: Start with the tallest bar, then check whether surrounding months leave enough liquidity spacing.
timeline.mobile_sheet_description: Open the month-by-month ladder schedule in a mobile-friendly sheet.
timeline.table_summary: Each row shows one maturity month, how many lots finish there, and how much net value returns.
timeline.peak_month_description: Use the peak month to see where the ladder bunches the most returning capital.
timeline.cluster_warning: This ladder is fairly concentrated in one maturity window.
timeline.cluster_ok: This ladder is still reasonably spread across maturity windows.
timeline.interpretation_description: A ladder is useful when it improves liquidity timing, not only when it grows faster. Check spacing before chasing higher totals.

multi_asset_page.page_notice_desc: This surface still has narrower historical coverage and should be treated as reference comparison only, not as a mature backtesting product.

optimizer_page.page_notice_desc: This page is assumption-sensitive and easy to over-read. Use it as a supporting scenario sorter after the core calculators, not as a primary decision surface.

notebook.title: My Portfolio / Notebook
notebook.subtitle: Track your real-world bond purchases and simulate their future value based on live data.
notebook.empty_desc: Create your first portfolio to start tracking your bond investments and managing your wealth.
notebook.no_lots: No active lots in this portfolio yet
notebook.load_error: Could not load portfolios. Please try again later.
notebook.create_error: Could not create a portfolio right now. Please try again later.
notebook.guest_desc: You can create and manage a local guest notebook even when sign-in is not configured.
notebook.demo_description: Example portfolio with multiple bond types
notebook.stored_portfolios_desc: Open one notebook at a time and use it as a working record of real bond lots.
notebook.scope_desc: Use the notebook to store real lots and revisit them later. It should support calculator work, not replace it with another dashboard.
notebook.current_lot_added: Current bond lot was added to the notebook.
notebook.import_failed: Import failed. Use the portfolio export JSON package.
notebook.workspace_intro: This should stay a clear portfolio workspace. Record the lots first, then inspect maturities, sharing, and exports.
notebook.portfolios_label_desc: Saved lot sets currently stored in the local notebook.
notebook.public_links_label_desc: Portfolios currently shared through a public link.
notebook.private_drafts_label_desc: Working portfolios that stay in the private notebook view.
notebook.stored_portfolios_note: Open one portfolio, review lots and the maturity window, and only then move into the aggregated projection.
notebook.record_intro_title: Open one portfolio, inspect the stored lots, and only then move into export or simulation.
notebook.record_intro_desc: Start with the stored lots and the upcoming liquidity window. The analytics tab should support the record, not turn it into a recommendation layer.
notebook.stored_lots_card_desc: Positions currently tracked in this portfolio record.
notebook.next_maturity_card_desc: Nearest upcoming maturity date across stored lots.
notebook.total_invested_desc: Nominal value across all stored lots.
notebook.public_share_desc: This portfolio currently has a public share link.
notebook.private_share_desc: This portfolio is currently kept private.
notebook.stored_lots_desc: Review each position as a record entry: bond type, amount, purchase date, and nominal value.
notebook.stored_lots_hint: Read the stored record first: bond type, purchase date, lot size, and nominal value.
notebook.liquidity_window_desc: See how much nominal cash returns inside a short upcoming maturity window.
notebook.usage_note_desc: Use this view to store and inspect lots. If you want to test reinvestment, early withdrawal, or scenario assumptions, jump into a calculator page with the linked lot details instead of turning the notebook into a planning dashboard.
notebook.projection_desc: Aggregated nominal value simulation across all stored lots. Read it after the records view, not before.
notebook.projection_read_desc: Treat this as a supporting projection of the stored record, not as a recommendation engine. Check the lot records and maturity window first.
notebook.projection_empty: Add lots to see the aggregated projection.
notebook.descriptive_desc: Portfolio pages should stay descriptive. They can summarize stored lots and simulated outcomes, but they should not frame one action as the recommended investor move.

create.title: Create one notebook
create.desc: Start with a clean portfolio name for the bond lots you actually hold.

store.title: Store real lots
store.desc: Record purchase dates, quantities, and bond types so later views stay grounded in real positions.

inspect.title: Inspect maturities
inspect.desc: Open the notebook later to review payouts, maturities, and exportable context.

notebook.tax_leak_action_required: Action Required: You have {count} large lots (e.g., {type} from {year}) totaling {value} outside a tax-free wrapper. Prioritize moving these to IKE/IKZE to avoid 19% Belka tax.
notebook.tax_leak_small: Small tax leak detected in {count} lots. Consider using IKE/IKZE for future purchases.
notebook.confirm_delete_portfolio_full: Delete portfolio "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

economic.subtitle: Historical context of Polish inflation and interest rates.

reference_copy.unavailable_description: This page does not yet have enough synchronization metadata to describe freshness or coverage.
reference_copy.fallback_description: Treat these series as reference support until the sync pipeline restores fuller coverage.
reference_copy.stale_description: The data comes from a valid source, but its time window is no longer current enough for a live reading.
reference_copy.synced_description: This series is backed by the synchronized data pipeline and can support calculator interpretation.
reference_copy.cpi_partial_reference: Monthly CPI still has only partial reference coverage. Do not read it as fully current market data.
reference_copy.cpi_fallback_reference: This CPI chart is using fallback coverage. Treat it as supporting context only until the official import is restored.
reference_copy.cpi_stale_coverage: The CPI source is valid, but the latest imported point is too old for this range to count as current.
reference_copy.reference_synced_context: Coverage is current enough to support calculator reading with confidence.
reference_copy.nbp_fallback_reference: The reference-rate history relies on a curated range backed by official NBP publications. Treat it as reference context, not a full live API stream.
reference_copy.nbp_partial_reference: NBP rate history is only partially synchronized, so missing points were filled from a curated historical range.
reference_copy.nbp_synced_context: Reference-rate history is synchronized and can support treasury-bond result interpretation.

economic.page_intro: This page provides calm macro context for bond calculators. It should not pretend to be a separate forecasting product or a large market dashboard.
economic.macro_support_title: Macro data should help read bond calculators, not distract from them.
economic.usage_guide_1: Inflation helps explain the real purchasing power of the result and the behavior of inflation-indexed bonds.
economic.usage_guide_2: NBP reference rate mainly provides context for ROR and DOR and for reading the broader rate environment.
economic.usage_guide_3: Current bond offer rate and NBP reference rate are not the same thing: for example, ROR may start from the issued offer rate and only later move to NBP plus margin.
economic.usage_guide_4: Shorter ranges improve readability. Wider ranges add historical context, but they do not replace missing data quality.
economic.data_quality_description: If synchronization is incomplete, the page should say that directly. These charts should help interpret calculator results, not create a false sense of completeness.
economic.page_scope_description: This is not a separate forecasting panel. The data here should serve as calm background for reading bond calculator outputs.
economic.inflation_desc: Year-over-year change in the price of goods and services.
economic.inflation_impact_desc: Inflation erodes the purchasing power of your money. If your investment return is lower than inflation, you are losing money in real terms.
economic.source_desc: Historical data provided by Statistics Poland (GUS) and the National Bank of Poland (NBP).
economic.nbp_rate_desc: Historical reference rate set by the National Bank of Poland.
economic.inflation_scale_notice: Readable mode caps the early spike so the rest of the series stays readable. Peak visible history exceeds {max}%.

fallback_notices.nbp_warning: Using fallback NBP reference data. Read the reference page more cautiously.
fallback_notices.nbp_live: NBP reference data comes from the synced reference dataset.
fallback_notices.inflation_warning: Using fallback CPI reference data. Read the reference page more cautiously.
fallback_notices.inflation_live: CPI reference data comes from the synced reference dataset.

comparison.desc_bond_vs_bond: Compare different Polish Treasury Bonds under the same conditions with automatic rollover.
comparison.comparison_desc: Comparison based on automatic renewal at maturity.
comparison.market_vs_bonds_desc: Historical simulator: Compare asset performance with monthly contributions adjusted for inflation.
comparison.inflation_loss_context: Due to inflation, your capital lost nearly {percent}% of its effective buying power.
comparison.savings_context_desc: "Savings Account" interest is not unified. It varies heavily between banks. This model assumes an average retail rate based on historical NBP margins (approx. 0.5-2%).
comparison.desc_independent: Compare two fully independent bond strategies with their own horizon, rollover, and tax assumptions.
comparison.shared_base_desc: Keep one shared base scenario, then apply scenario-specific overrides only when they truly matter.
comparison.shared_base_scope: The left panel defines amount, purchase date, shared horizon, macro assumptions, and default tax wrapper for both scenarios.
comparison.shared_tax_desc: Leave the shared tax wrapper in place unless one scenario really needs its own tax treatment.
comparison.custom_horizon_desc: Override the shared timing for this scenario only.
comparison.shared_horizon_desc: This shared horizon remains the default timing path until a scenario explicitly overrides it.
comparison.tax_override_desc: Use a tax override only when this scenario intentionally departs from the shared wrapper.
comparison.horizon_override_desc: This override changes the end date for this scenario only. The shared base still governs the other side.
comparison.family_override_note: Family-bond overrides stay available, but only make sense if the household eligibility condition really applies.
comparison.base_follows_shared_desc: Amount, purchase date, inflation, NBP path, and shared horizon stay aligned unless you open optional overrides.
comparison.optional_overrides_desc: Open only when one scenario should intentionally diverge from the shared base.
comparison.historical_drawdown_desc: Maximum drops from peak value during market crashes.

verdict.higher_real_value: currently shows the higher real value
verdict.higher_net_payout: currently shows the higher modeled net payout
verdict.caution_text: Treat this as one scenario read, not a universal bond ranking. If assumptions change, the gap can change too.

comparison.table_desc: Plain year-by-year value check. Use it to inspect where one path is ahead at a given point, not as a recommendation signal.
comparison.table_mobile_desc: On smaller screens, compare the scenarios as a list of checkpoints instead of a wide table.
comparison.table_desktop_note: The table confirms which scenario is ahead in a specific period.
comparison.table_footer_note: The badge shows which path is ahead in that specific row only. Later rows can reverse earlier leads.
comparison.ready_title: Compare two committed scenarios, not two moving targets.
comparison.ready_desc: Set the shared base first, change scenario overrides only if needed, then run one clean comparison.
comparison.ready_shared_base_desc: Amount, dates, inflation path, and tax wrapper.
comparison.ready_overrides_desc: Bond type and optional per-scenario adjustments.
comparison.ready_committed_desc: Run comparison, then inspect snapshot, chart, and table.
comparison.ready_footer: Run the comparison from the fixed calculate control in the bottom-right corner.
comparison.stale_results: Inputs changed. Results below are stale until you rerun the comparison.
comparison.chart_header_desc: Chart points are aligned by actual calendar date so both scenarios share one time axis.
comparison.chart_help_desc: Check the end level of both scenarios first. Only then inspect the rhythm of changes and flat stretches.
comparison.comparison_chart_help_title: How to read the comparison chart
comparison.comparison_chart_help_desc: Use the chart to spot the scenario shape. Payout cadence and update rhythm stay available on demand, not as permanent noise.
comparison.comparison_chart_help_note: Read the final level of both scenarios first. Only then inspect slope changes and flat stretches.
comparison.mixed_cadence_notice: This comparison mixes payout cadences: {bondTypeA} updates {cadenceA}, while {bondTypeB} updates {cadenceB}.
comparison.end_level_desc: Shows which scenario finishes higher under the same assumptions.
comparison.update_rhythm_desc: Flat segments do not automatically mean stagnation. They can simply reflect slower crediting or payout timing.
comparison.assumptions_meta_desc: Both scenarios keep explicit assumptions and warnings, but they should not dominate the outcome and table.
comparison.page_notice_desc: Use this page to compare two modeled outcomes under explicit assumptions. It is best treated as a scenario check, not as a universal ranking or recommendation engine.

reading_guide.understand_assumptions: This comparison shows scenario tradeoffs, not a universally best bond.
reading_guide.compare_payout: Compare the final payout first, then inspect taxes, cadence, and real value.
reading_guide.check_real_value: Turn on real-value mode when inflation protection matters more than nominal cash.

page.configuration_description: One amount, one horizon, and one assumption set. This keeps the comparison focused on bond-structure differences.
page.real_value_toggle_description: Show the purchasing-power-adjusted result instead of nominal wealth.
page.rollover_description: If the horizon outlasts a bond term, the engine chains the next purchase cycle automatically.
page.bond_picker_description: Pick only the bonds you actually want to test inside the same shared scenario.
page.how_to_read_description: Start from the winner, then check cadence, taxes, and inflation-adjusted value.

steps.configure_description: Keep the same capital, horizon, and assumptions so only bond structure changes.
steps.run_description: Recalculate after edits so all scenarios use the same current assumptions.
steps.decide_description: Check final payout first, then inspect taxes, cadence, and real value.

page.empty_state_description: Pick the bonds first, then commit the shared assumptions, then run one clean comparison.

empty_steps.choose_bonds_description: Select the products you want to test side by side.
empty_steps.set_assumptions_description: Use one capital base, one horizon, and one CPI/NBP view before comparing outputs.
empty_steps.run_description: Use the fixed recalculate control after the shared setup is ready.

page.summary_description: A quick winner view before you inspect each bond card and the full chart.
page.results_description: Each card keeps the same scenario base so the differences come from the bond design.
page.chart_description: Use the chart to compare path shape, not only the final payout.
page.chart_note_title: How to read the comparison chart
page.chart_note_description: Read the final level of each path first. Then inspect slope changes and flat stretches.
page.calculation_context_description: Warnings, assumptions, and data freshness for the shared comparison engine.

controls.description: Set one historical entry point, one initial amount, and one monthly contribution before recalculating the comparison.
controls.advanced_description: Reveal the shared timeline entry and purchasing-power switch only when you need them.

regular_form.contribution_help: The amount you plan to invest periodically.
regular_form.frequency_help: How often you plan to make a purchase. Monthly is the most common setup.
regular_form.start_date_help: When you begin your regular investment plan.
regular_form.withdrawal_date_help: When you plan to stop investing and withdraw the accumulated capital.
regular_form.horizon_help: How many years you plan to continue this periodic investment.
regular_form.initial_rate_help: For ROR and DOR, you can customize the starting rate used before later reference-rate updates.
regular_form.nbp_help: The National Bank of Poland reference rate used for ROR and DOR after the first period.
regular_form.inflation_help: Expected average inflation over the period. Used for interest projection and real purchasing power.
regular_form.margin_help: Guaranteed margin added to the indexed base rate for later bond periods.
regular_form.rebuy_help: Enable this when you plan to use the swap promotion that applies a small discount on new bonds.
regular_form.tax_help: You can adjust the standard 19% capital-gains tax rate here.

regular_summary.hero_description: This is the final projected value for the committed recurring plan. Review the summary cards first, then inspect yearly buckets and recent lots only if you need more detail.
regular_summary.hero_narrative: This is a scenario for one bond family and one contribution cadence. Treat it as a plan test, not as an automatic recommendation.
regular_summary.final_nominal_helper: Projected withdrawal value before inflation adjustment.
regular_summary.net_profit_helper: Net gain after tax and early withdrawal fees.
regular_summary.real_value_helper: Inflation-adjusted purchasing power at the end date.
regular_summary.real_cagr_helper: Annualized real return across the full plan.
regular_summary.tax_helper: Total tax assumed in the scenario.
regular_summary.yearly_description: Review how much capital each purchase year contributed to the final outcome.
regular_summary.yearly_mobile_description: On smaller screens, read yearly buckets as a list instead of a wide table.
regular_summary.recent_description: The latest purchases help verify timing, maturity dates, and per-lot net value.
regular_summary.recent_note: Start with the summary cards and yearly buckets. Individual lots are only a quick check of purchase timing and maturity dates.

ladder.description: Build a bond ladder to ensure recurring liquidity and steady growth.
ladder.what_is_desc: A bond ladder is a strategy where you buy bonds regularly. After the first full cycle, one bond can mature on a recurring schedule, giving you liquidity for spending or reinvestment.

market_assumptions_form.item_1: Simulates a lower CPI path after year one, so future indexed coupons rise more slowly.
market_assumptions_form.item_2: Uses the exact CPI path you selected after the first year.
market_assumptions_form.item_3: Simulates a higher CPI path after year one, so future indexed coupons rise faster.
market_assumptions_form.item_4: For indexed bonds, CPI changes future coupons after the opening period and also affects real-value readouts.
market_assumptions_form.item_5: For ROR and DOR, CPI does not change the coupon. Later coupons follow NBP, while inflation here is mainly for purchasing-power readouts.
market_assumptions_form.item_6: For this bond, inflation does not change the coupon. This assumption mainly drives purchasing-power and real-value display.
market_assumptions_form.item_8: Post-year-one CPI scenarios stay hidden because this bond does not reset its coupon with inflation. Only the real-value readout changes.
market_assumptions_form.item_9: This NBP path drives later periods after the issued opening offer. The first month still uses the active issued offer rate.

recalculate_button.item_2: Run the first clean calculation after setting your scenario.
recalculate_button.item_3: New inputs are staged. Recalculate only when the scenario is ready.

pdf_utils.item_11: This PDF is generated from a structured data summary rather than a page screenshot.
pdf_utils.item_16: This PDF uses the same normalized result layer as the scenario summary. It does not rely on DOM screenshots, color parsing, or chart capture.

nbprate_chart.item_1: This series still relies on fallback coverage. Read it as reference context until sync restores fuller history.
nbprate_chart.item_2: This series is synced and can support calculator interpretation without taking over the page.

inflation_chart.item_1: This chart is using fallback data. Treat it as supporting context, not as full historical coverage.
inflation_chart.item_2: This series is synced and can act as calm context for reading calculator results.

portfolio_details.item_2: Nominal value across all stored lots.
portfolio_details.item_6: This portfolio currently has a public share link.
portfolio_details.item_7: This portfolio is currently kept private.

notebook_container.item_3: Import failed. Use the portfolio export JSON package.
notebook_container.item_6: This should be a simple portfolio notebook, not another dashboard. Record the lots first, then inspect maturities and exports.
notebook_container.item_9: Saved lot sets currently stored in the local notebook.
notebook_container.item_11: Portfolios currently shared through a public link.
notebook_container.item_13: Working portfolios that stay in the private notebook view.
notebook_container.item_14: Open one portfolio, review lots and the maturity window, and only then move into the aggregated projection.

bond_timing_section.item_3: The horizon outlasts this bond, so the calculator automatically chains follow-on cycles instead of asking for a separate toggle.
bond_timing_section.item_4: The horizon stays within one native cycle, so the result shows a single purchase plus any early-exit fee if needed.

bond_config_section.item_3: Use the currently active offer definition

saved_scenarios_panel.item_2: Resume, duplicate, or clean up your last planning runs.
saved_scenarios_panel.item_4: No saved scenarios yet. Save current inputs to build reusable planning paths.

bond_timeline.item_5: Each row is a scenario checkpoint, not a separate purchase. Read it left to right: when it happens, what it represents, which rate basis it used, and what value it leaves at that point.
bond_timeline.item_8: On smaller screens, read the schedule as a sequence of checkpoints instead of a wide table.

bond_comparison_container.item_2: This comparison shows scenario tradeoffs, not a universally best bond.
bond_comparison_container.item_3: Read it as a test of one committed shared setup, not as finished advice for every investor.
bond_comparison_container.item_4: Read the main result first, then the bond cards, and open the meta context only when you need to verify assumptions.
bond_comparison_container.item_6: One amount, one horizon, and one assumption set. This keeps the comparison focused on bond structure differences.
bond_comparison_container.item_9: Switch the chart and reading into real-value mode.
bond_comparison_container.item_11: Rollover is inferred automatically for each bond when the shared horizon outlasts its native term.
bond_comparison_container.item_13: Pick only the bonds you actually want to test inside the same shared scenario.
bond_comparison_container.item_15: This page shows the tradeoffs of one shared scenario. Interpretation notes stay explicit, but they should not overshadow the main result.
bond_comparison_container.item_19: Check the best net payout or real-value outcome first.
bond_comparison_container.item_21: Then compare profit, real CAGR, and tax for the same horizon.
bond_comparison_container.item_23: Only then open the meta context if you need to verify assumptions or data quality.
bond_comparison_container.item_25: Pick the bonds first, then commit the shared assumptions, then run one clean comparison.
bond_comparison_container.item_32: Inputs changed. The results below still show the previous run until you recalculate.
bond_comparison_container.item_34: Start with the scenario winner and the main gap in net payout.
bond_comparison_container.item_38: Each card shows the outcome for the same amount and the same horizon.
bond_comparison_container.item_40: Use the chart to inspect the scenario path, not to manufacture winner drama.
bond_comparison_container.item_42: Check the peak month and final level first. Only then compare the path shapes.
bond_comparison_container.item_43: Assumptions, warnings, and data context stay explicit, but they are support material after the main result.

comparison_controls.item_1: Set one historical entry point, one initial amount, and one monthly contribution before recalculating the comparison.
comparison_controls.item_3: Start year, month, presets, and inflation-adjusted display.

scenario_override_card.item_3: The engine enables rollover automatically when the scenario horizon outlasts this bond’s native term.

retirement_planner_container.item_2: Run one narrow withdrawal scenario with explicit assumptions and review the balance path before treating it as planning evidence.
retirement_planner_container.item_4: Set capital, withdrawal, horizon, and supported bond family. Advanced rate and wrapper assumptions stay collapsed below.
retirement_planner_container.item_10: Inflation, modeled NBP rate, and tax wrapper.
retirement_planner_container.item_14: Use the floating action in the lower corner when this withdrawal scenario is ready for its first run or for recalculation.
retirement_planner_container.item_15: Inputs changed. The balance path below still shows the previous committed run. Recalculate when you want to refresh it.
retirement_planner_container.item_20: No projected depletion inside the selected horizon.
retirement_planner_container.item_22: Nominal end-of-horizon balance under this one modeled path.
retirement_planner_container.item_24: Cumulative withdrawals inside the selected horizon.
retirement_planner_container.item_28: This chart shows the committed scenario only. It is sampled yearly to keep the path readable.
retirement_planner_container.item_32: Start with scenario status and final balance. Use the chart to see the depletion path over time, not as a full retirement-planning recommendation.
retirement_planner_container.item_36: Open this when you want the supporting assumption trail behind the current withdrawal run.
retirement_planner_container.item_40: No extra assumptions were reported beyond the visible inputs.
retirement_planner_container.item_41: No extra warnings were returned for this scenario.
retirement_planner_container.item_43: Ready to test one withdrawal path?
retirement_planner_container.item_44: Set the starting capital, withdrawal amount, horizon, and supported bond family. Then run one committed calculation before reading the balance path.
retirement_planner_container.item_48: This is a steady-rate depletion model, not a complete retirement advice engine.
retirement_planner_container.item_49: Advanced assumptions stay available, but the first reading should come from one clean committed run.
retirement_planner_container.item_51: Open this when you want the constraints behind this withdrawal surface.
retirement_planner_container.item_53: This run projects depletion on {date}. Re-run with a lower withdrawal, longer horizon check, or different supported bond family before drawing conclusions.
retirement_planner_container.item_57: Uses one steady modeled annual rate rather than changing market paths over time.
retirement_planner_container.item_58: Does not model changing withdrawals, rolling ladders, or broader retirement advice.

ladder_container.item_1: This surface is for schedule clarity, not bond selection advice.
ladder_container.item_2: Focus first on maturity spacing and cash-return concentration.
ladder_container.item_4: Start with the liquidity summary and concentration check. Only then move into the month-by-month table.
ladder_container.item_6: Assumptions and supporting data stay available, but they should not overshadow the schedule readout.

ladder_timeline.item_4: Peak maturity month first. Then read the coverage window and concentration check to see whether cash returns are spread evenly or clustered too tightly.
ladder_timeline.item_5: A ladder is not only about the final outcome. It is about when and how densely cash returns.
ladder_timeline.item_8: Average net cash that returns in each maturity month.
ladder_timeline.item_10: Positive values mean average maturity cash is above the typical lot contribution.
ladder_timeline.item_12: First and last maturity month visible in the current scenario.
ladder_timeline.item_14: Use the chart for spacing, then confirm exact month totals in the table below.
ladder_timeline.item_16: This chart shows the maturity spread. Use it to inspect density and clustering, not to hunt for one dramatic month.
ladder_timeline.item_19: On smaller screens, read maturity months as a list instead of a wide table.
ladder_timeline.item_25: The table confirms the exact lot count and cash total in each maturity month.
ladder_timeline.item_32: If most maturities cluster in one period, the ladder is less even than it looks from a single top-line return number.
ladder_timeline.item_36: This ladder is meaningfully clustered. Liquidity arrives in fewer windows than a smooth ladder would suggest.
ladder_timeline.item_37: Maturities are reasonably spread across the current timeline.
ladder_timeline.item_39: 1. Check coverage. 2. Check peak month. 3. Check concentration. 4. Confirm exact month totals in the table.

regular_investment_calculator_container.item_1: This is a recurring purchase plan, so the result depends on lot count, timing, and bond type.
regular_investment_calculator_container.item_2: The chart helps you read the pace of capital formation, not just the endpoint.
regular_investment_calculator_container.item_3: Assumptions and data-quality flags should stay secondary to the main outcome.
regular_investment_calculator_container.item_5: Start with the final outcome and growth pace, then move into the assumptions and meta details only if needed.
regular_investment_calculator_container.item_8: The chart shows how invested capital and projected portfolio value evolve over time.
regular_investment_calculator_container.item_9: Assumptions and supporting data remain visible, but they should not overshadow the plan itself.

regular_investment_inputs_form.item_1: Family-bond scenarios are only meaningful if the household eligibility condition really applies.

page.item_1: These pages remain reachable, but they should not compete visually with the core product. This is a side lab, not the front-door promise of the app.
page.item_3: Open this only when you intentionally want to inspect a narrower or more assumption-sensitive scenario.

item_4: One primary calculation at a time
Strategies only after the baseline result
landing_dashboard_client.item_7: Saved scenarios should act as a quick resume point, not a separate dashboard.

item_8.title: Learn the rules
item_8.description: Start with education or go straight to the single calculator if you already know the bond type and horizon.

item_8.title: Run one calculation
item_8.description: Enter one committed scenario, calculate it cleanly, and read the result without dashboard side panels.

item_8.title: Expand only after that
item_8.description: Comparison, recurring investing, ladder strategy, and notebook should stay secondary, not first.

landing_dashboard_client.item_9: No local saved scenarios yet. The home page should not pretend to be a control center when nothing is saved.
landing_dashboard_client.item_11: This page should route you into the main tools while keeping the first step calm and obvious.
landing_dashboard_client.item_13: These pages remain available, but they should be visited only after the primary scenario flow.
landing_dashboard_client.item_14: Start with one clean calculation before moving into comparisons, notebook work, or side scenarios.

page.item_3: Use this page to test maturity spacing, rollover timing, and cashflow shape. It is a scenario tool for one ladder setup, not a broad strategy verdict.

retirement.page_notice_desc: This page uses a simplified steady-rate depletion model. It is useful for narrow withdrawal checks, but it is not a full retirement planning engine. Supported bond families here: {supportedBondTypes}.
retirement.supported_bonds_limit: Supports only {bondTypes} in this reduced-scope surface.
retirement.ready_step_balance_desc: Start from {initialCapital} and one fixed withdrawal amount.
retirement.ready_step_horizon_desc: The current run is staged for {horizon}.

shared_portfolio_page.public_notice: You are viewing a shared public portfolio.

reading_guide.follow_contribution: Check how each contribution gets less time to grow when it enters later in the plan.
reading_guide.compare_lot_age: Compare early and late lots before focusing on the final total.
reading_guide.check_real_value: Use real-value mode when inflation context matters more than nominal totals.

regular_investment_page.how_to_read_description: Read contribution timing first, then chart shape, then the calculation context.
regular_investment_page.chart_description: The chart shows how the plan builds over time under one repeated contribution rule.
regular_investment_page.calculation_context_description: Warnings, assumptions, and freshness context for the recurring-purchase engine.
regular_investment_page.form_description: Main contribution plan first. Advanced assumptions stay collapsed until needed.
regular_investment_page.core_plan_description: Set bond type, tax wrapper, and repeating contribution amount.
regular_investment_page.family_bond_note: Family bonds have eligibility rules. Use them here only when the purchase context really qualifies.
